backingDescription: GFI is a protocol/governance token and is not a direct redeemable claim on a specific credit portfolio, fund share, reserve account, or collateral pool. Goldfinch Prime is described separately in official docs as providing private-credit exposure through Heron-issued contingent payment notes backed by equity interests in third-party private-credit funds. That product-level structure should not be treated as native GFI backing.

_meta.classificationNote: Reserve layer is intentionally conservative. Goldfinch Prime private-credit exposure is product-level context, while GFI itself has no verified reserve, custodian, PoR oracle, or redemption asset.
needsFurtherReview: Verify whether any official document treats GFI as having economic rights to protocol revenue, pools, reserves, or collateral.
If Goldfinch Prime is modeled as a separate Nexus asset, research Heron entities, note structure, underlying BDC/private-credit fund exposure, service providers, and redemption mechanics separately.
